name: 5q13_2_SMN1_SMN2_SMA_locus
description: 5q13.2 spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) locus. A ~500 kb inverted segmental duplication contains the SMN1 (telomeric) and SMN2 (centromeric) paralogs at >99.9% identity, plus paralog copies of NAIP, GTF2H2/GTF2H2C, and SERF1A/SERF1B. SMN1 vs SMN2 differ by a single base (c.840 C>T in exon 7) that disrupts splicing. Homozygous SMN1 loss causes SMA; SMN2 copy number modifies severity. The locus is the textbook paralog-mapping challenge.

architecture.internal_structure: Inverted duplication of two ~500 kb half-blocks; SMN1/SMN2 differ by 5 paralog-specific variants (c.840 C>T being functional); NAIP / GTF2H2 / SERF1 paralog pairs flank. Population SMN2 copy number 0-4 (mean ~2).
